reasoning: The health bar (bar_health_frame_v05) appears as a wide horizontal strip roughly 220px wide x 25px tall in the composited scene. The player entity in the lower-left area is approximately 32x32px. As a HUD bar element, its on-screen footprint is acceptable in isolation, but here it is composited directly into the game world space near the player and scene entities, where it spans nearly a third of the scene width. This makes it feel like a floating UI overlay rather than a diegetic or well-proportioned world element. The bar is wider than the player character, violating the scale hierarchy. Its flat, elongated silhouette clashes with the top-down tile perspective — it reads as a 2D UI widget dropped into 3D-perspective tile space rather than a toy-diorama prop. The golden frame detail is visible but the extreme horizontal stretch overwhelms nearby 32x32 entities and disrupts the scene's compact diorama feel, pushing it toward 'wallpaper' territory.
